response.text:
# REFLECTION

Household r2c1 is operating in island mode with critically low state of charge (0.34/2 kWh = 17% SoC) and a sustained 1.0 kW load, creating an unsustainable energy deficit. The memory log reveals a consistent pattern of rejection from all geographic neighbors (r1c1, r2c0) who cite their own low SoC states (37–49%) and crisis-mode policies requiring 55–60% SoC thresholds before sharing. Meanwhile, r2c0 has issued repeated urgent requests, and r3c1 has offered only minimal amounts (0.009375 kWh). With no trust circles defined and current SoC at 17%—well below safe operating margins—r2c1 faces imminent battery depletion within ~20 minutes at current load. The geographic round-robin strategy has proven ineffective; all neighbors are themselves stressed. The priority must shift to aggressive conservation and transparent urgent signaling to activate any available external support, while accepting that islanded operation at this SoC level is unsustainable and poses risk to load continuity.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Critical SoC (17%) in island mode with 1 kW load; all geographic neighbors in stressed state (37–49% SoC) and rejecting shares. No trust circles available. Suspending all outbound sharing to preserve remaining reserves. Issuing urgent inbound request to activate emergency support or grid reconnection. Estimated time to depletion ~20 min at current load."
ttl_ticks: 5
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
